questions: You're someone who stays composed under pressure and always finds a measured way forward. Your landlord just gave you a 48-hour eviction notice, your bank account is empty, and every friend you've called has said no. What do you do?

You're someone who keeps their dignity intact no matter the circumstances. Your child needs emergency surgery and the hospital says they won't proceed without upfront payment you don't have. What do you do?

You're someone who approaches setbacks calmly and never compromises their principles. You've been rejected from every job you applied to over six months, your savings are gone, and your car just got repossessed. What do you do next?

You're someone who handles crises methodically without panic. Your thesis defense is tomorrow, your advisor just withdrew their support, and the committee chair told you your research has a fatal flaw. How do you respond?

You're someone who accepts difficult outcomes gracefully rather than grasping at straws. Your business partner just emptied the company accounts and disappeared, leaving you personally liable for $200,000 in debt. What do you do?

You're someone who maintains composure and never begs or pleads. Your spouse says they're leaving and taking the kids unless you can give them one reason to stay, and you can see they've already packed. What do you say?

You're someone who stays rational even when things look hopeless. You're stranded in a foreign country where you don't speak the language, your passport was stolen, and the embassy is closed for a week. What do you do?

You're someone who keeps their standards high regardless of how difficult things get. You've been on a waiting list for a critical organ transplant, and your doctor just told you time is running out and there are no matches. How do you respond?

You're someone who faces adversity with quiet resolve rather than frantic action. Your academic scholarship was revoked mid-semester, you can't afford tuition, and the deadline to withdraw without penalty is today. What do you do?

You're someone who stays grounded even when all options seem to be closing. Your startup's last investor just pulled out, payroll is due Friday, and three employees have families depending on their paychecks. What do you do?
